summary: Front price ($3.245) sits 3.3pp above its seasonal median and about 11% above modeled fair value ($2.929, band $2.429-$3.429), though the seasonality read carries LOW reliability (51pp 10-90% spread). Cooling demand is running hot for the season - +6.6% vs 5y in days 1-7 and +13.2% in days 8-16, with total demand trending +11% above normal - supporting incremental gas burn into peak summer. Managed money is net short 82.7k contracts, only the 18th percentile of the last 5 years, an unusually crowded short that raises squeeze risk. That bullish backdrop is offset by a larger-than-forecast storage build (+87 Bcf vs +74 Bcf expected, a +13 Bcf bearish surprise) leaving stocks +175 Bcf above the 5-year average, and by stretched short-term technicals (5m RSI 83.8, price above the upper Bollinger band on 5m/15m/1h) even as daily/weekly trend is only neutral-to-bearish. Net: modest bullish tilt over 3-12 weeks from weather/COT/seasonality, but near-term price is rich vs fair value and overbought, arguing for patience before adding length.

longTerm.entry: $3.05-$3.15 (buy dips toward spot fair value / next-month price)
longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; trim size given LOW seasonality reliability
longTerm.rationale: Cooling demand is running 6.6-13.2% above 5y normal and rising into peak season, COT shows managed money at an 18th-percentile net-short extreme (squeeze risk), and the fair-value gap narrows from +$0.316 now to +$0.163 by +21D as fair value drifts up toward price. Seasonality also favors Sep strength (63% probability higher at 4wks), though reliability is LOW.
longTerm.invalidation: Repeated bearish storage surprises widen the 5y surplus further, cooling demand rolls back below 5y normal, or price closes back below the $2.85 fair-value support.

shortTerm.rationale: Near-term weather demand is bullish (+6.6% vs 5y, rangePos 0.72) but price is already 'slightly over' fair value now ($3.245 vs $2.929 fair, top third of band) and short-term technicals are stretched - 5m RSI 83.8 with price above the upper Bollinger band on 5m/15m/1h - while EMA crosses on 15m/1h remain bearish (50<200). This mix argues against both chasing longs and fading a still-bullish demand backdrop.
shortTerm.invalidation: A clean close above $3.35 (fresh highs, momentum continuation) or a break below $2.85 (fair-value support fails) would resolve the current indecision and define the next directional trade.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for a pullback to $2.95-$3.05 with hourly RSI cooling below 60 (buy-the-dip setup), or a confirmed close above $3.35 (momentum continuation setup).

keyDrivers: Cooling demand +6.6%/+13.2% above 5y normal (1-7D/8-16D) and trending up
Managed-money net short at 18th percentile of 5y range - crowded short, squeeze risk
Front price ~11% above fair value ($3.245 vs $2.929), but gap narrows to +$0.163 by +21D
Storage surplus +175 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish +13 Bcf build surprise caps upside
Seasonality favors strength into Sep but reliability is LOW (51pp spread)
risks: Low seasonality reliability (51pp 10-90% spread) means the seasonal edge could easily fail
Short-term technicals are overbought (5m RSI 83.8, price above upper Bollinger) - pullback risk
Persistent storage surplus vs 5y average is a bearish overhang if builds keep beating forecast
disclaimer: Informational only, not financial advice. Auto-generated hourly.
